title: heteromorpha bojeriana
intro: Heteromorpha bojeriana, also known as the forest elder, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Madagascar and other parts of East Africa. It is a shrub or small tree with a rich history of traditional use, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and roots are used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as to boost the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. In traditional medicine, the forest elder is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in topical applications, to harness its health benefits.
